label: Skitters
description: This individual is a diminutive goblin of ratlike aspect, marked by bright_green skin and a narrow, wedge-shaped face. Yellow eyes sit deep beneath a heavy brow ridge, lending a fixed, watchful stare. Black hair grows in uneven tufts along the crown and nape, framing prominent ears and a blunt, dented nose. The body is wiry and compact, with long fingers and stained nails suited to squeezing through stone cracks and gnawing at refuse.

info.personality: A carrion-minded scavenger habituated to salt-stone tunnels and human refuse, driven by food, concealment, and opportunistic theft. It avoids open confrontation, relying on abrupt darts into darkness and a practiced stillness when threatened. It returns to reliable caches and learns routines quickly, treating the grotto as a mapped territory of smells, gaps, and safe ledges.

relationships.context: Both are grotto-born, food-driven scavengers using the same tunnel network and hidden caches, making competition over refuse and safe routes likely.
Shares the same territory and food sources in the Saltwrit Grotto; Skitters’ opportunistic theft/scavenging would naturally bring it into conflict with a territory-bound rat.
Overlapping circuits, bolt-holes, and caches in the same grotto make frequent competition and skirmishes likely.
Co-located in the Saltwrit Grotto; Skitters likely recognizes and avoids this routine-bound, territorial undead as a recurring hazard.
Both inhabit the grotto; Iscel’s boundary-enforcement makes it a predictable presence Skitters would learn to evade.
Shared location and Ivo’s patrol/territorial behavior would make it a known obstacle in Skitters’ mapped routes.
Same site; Nareth’s repetitive labor and attention to disturbances would make it a familiar, avoidable presence to a stealth scavenger.
Co-inhabitants of the grotto; Ostel’s intrusion-triggered violence makes it a recognized threat Skitters would track and avoid.
Both present in the grotto; Oswin’s task-bound, tool-like undead behavior would be a recurrent feature of Skitters’ environment rather than a personal bond.
